---
title: "Interactive Shiny Application"
subtitle: "Investigate the TenantThread breach through four analytical lenses"
toc: true
---

The centrepiece of this project is an interactive **R Shiny** dashboard that turns our analysis into an exploratory investigation tool. Filter the data, follow the timeline, trace the communication network, and weigh the evidence for each competing hypothesis.

::: {.btn-row}
<a class="btn-doc solid" href="https://wujiayan.shinyapps.io/VAST_Challenge_2026" target="_blank">🚀 Open in a new tab</a>
<a class="btn-doc" href="https://github.com/" target="_blank">💻 Source: combined_app.R</a>
:::

## Live application

The app is deployed on **shinyapps.io**. If the frame below is blank, the instance may be spinning up from sleep — give it a few seconds, or open it directly using the button above.

::: {.app-frame}
<div class="bar"><span class="dot" style="background:#ff5f56"></span><span class="dot" style="background:#ffbd2e"></span><span class="dot" style="background:#27c93f"></span>&nbsp; wujiayan.shinyapps.io/VAST_Challenge_2026</div>
<iframe src="https://wujiayan.shinyapps.io/VAST_Challenge_2026" loading="lazy" title="VAST Challenge 2026 Shiny Application"></iframe>
:::

## What's inside

The dashboard is organised into four tabs, mirroring the project's analytical objectives:

::: {.card-grid}

::: {.feature-card}
<div class="ic blue">🗂️</div>
### Overview
<p>Orientation to the case, the dataset, and the two competing hypotheses that frame the investigation.</p>
:::

::: {.feature-card}
<div class="ic teal">⏱️</div>
### Timeline &amp; Leak
<p>The minute-by-minute breach window (4:00–6:00 PM, 5 June), highlighting the social-media leak and the embargo lift.</p>
:::

::: {.feature-card}
<div class="ic amber">🕸️</div>
### Agent &amp; Communication Flow
<p>The communication network between the seven AI agents, with centrality metrics to surface key coordinators and bottlenecks.</p>
:::

::: {.feature-card}
<div class="ic purple">⚖️</div>
### Evidence &amp; Judgment
<p>A competing-hypotheses board that systematically weighs evidence for a deliberate leak versus a system breakdown.</p>
:::

:::

## Built with

The application is written in R and assembled from the project's three task modules (`task1.R`, `task2.R`, `task3.R`) into a single `combined_app.R`. Key packages include:

- **Interactive dashboard** — `shiny`, `shinydashboard`, `bslib`, `DT`
- **Network analysis** — `tidygraph`, `igraph`, `ggraph`, `visNetwork`
- **Text analysis** — `tidytext`, `quanteda`, `wordcloud`
- **Visualisation** — `ggplot2`, `plotly`, `lubridate`, `tidyverse`

## Run it locally

```r
# Clone the repository, then from the "Shiny app" folder:
install.packages(c(
  "shiny", "shinydashboard", "bslib", "DT", "tidyverse",
  "tidygraph", "igraph", "ggraph", "visNetwork",
  "tidytext", "plotly", "lubridate"
))

shiny::runApp("combined_app.R")
```
